fix: add missing tg_user_* argtypes/restype in binding.py

Without argtypes=[c_void_p], ctypes truncates the 64-bit pointer to
32 bits when passing user_ptr to tg_user_id/tg_user_first_name etc.
This produced a garbage pointer and caused a segfault in User.__init__.

- tg_user_first_name (restype=c_char_p)
- tg_user_last_name  (restype=c_char_p)

fix(c): implement proper HTML/Markdown formatting via parseTextEntities

Previously parse_mode was put inside inputMessageText as an unknown field
(TDLib ignores it), so all formatting was silently dropped.

Changes:
- tg_internal.h: add TG_FMTTEXT_MAX = TG_TEXT_MAX*6 for formatted text buffers
- tg_message.c: add _json_str_escape() to safely embed text in JSON
- tg_message.c: rewrite _build_text_content() to call td_execute() with
  parseTextEntities (documented as synchronous in TDLib) for html/markdown
  modes; the returned formattedText (with entities array) is embedded
  directly in inputMessageText — identical to how Pyrogram/Telethon work
- tg_message.c: update tg_send_text_ex/tg_edit_text_ex to use
  _build_text_content() instead of duplicating the broken logic
- tg_message.c: increase all send/edit buffers from TG_TEXT_MAX to
  TG_FMTTEXT_MAX to accommodate entity-rich responses
- Rebuilt libtg.so

Scan deeper candidate windows for distributed claims

on objects), a small claim window can return empty and leave workers polling
idle despite remaining backlog. Expand jobs/claim to scan multiple bounded
candidate windows in one request, and add a regression test that ensures an
eligible newer faces job is still leased.

fix: cap WASM result rows in fetchContentAtomsByHubs (stack overflow)

Large corpora with 200 hub compounds × 100+ molecules each would return
10,000+ rows through a single PGlite query. Even though only IDs were
selected (~20 bytes/row), 10K rows ≈ 200KB marshaled through WASM stack
— enough to trigger 'Maximum call stack size exceeded'.

Two-part fix:
1. Add PGLITE_CHUNK_RESULT_IDS = 500 — max rows returned per fan-out
   ID query (distinct from CHUNK_IDS which caps IN-param count).
2. Per-chunk LIMIT = min(remaining, PGLITE_CHUNK_RESULT_IDS) so each
   query returns at most 500 ID rows (~10KB), iterating chunks until
   maxCount is reached.

Result: illuminate-global on a 370K+ atom corpus will iterate safely
in ~20 chunks of 500 IDs each to hit maxNodes=10000.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Migrate Copilot customization validation to skills-ref (#327)

## Summary

This branch consolidates Copilot customization validation around the
`validate_agent_files` package and aligns skill validation with
`skills-ref` as the authoritative first gate. It also finishes the
migration away from the older prompt-era validation structure by
removing legacy internal modules and renaming the Python package to
match the canonical CLI.

## Changes

### Refactoring

- Unify the validator CLI under `validate_agent_files`
- Merge the old split validator internals into one top-level package
layout
- Remove legacy `validate_skills` and `validate_customizations` internal
modules after consolidation
- Rename the Python package from `validate_agents` to
`validate_agent_files`

### Features

- Make `skills-ref` the primary validator for skill files
- Preserve repository-specific checks for duplicate names,
cross-references, agent handoffs, and prompt `#file:` references after
upstream validation passes

### Documentation and Configuration

- Update validator package docs for the new package name and validation
model
- Update workflow, pre-commit, requirements, and Ruff first-party
configuration to the renamed package path
- Fix two skill frontmatter descriptions that were invalid under
stricter YAML validation

## Technical Details

- The validator package now lives under
`py_packages/validate_agent_files`
- The canonical console entry remains `validate_agent_files`
- Skill validation now runs `skills-ref` first and returns immediately
on upstream validation errors
- Repository-specific validation remains layered on top once the
upstream skill specification passes

## Testing Strategy

### Focused Tests

Ran:

```bash
python -m pytest \
  py_packages/validate_agent_files/tests/test_cli.py \
  py_packages/validate_agent_files/tests/validate_customizations/test_entrypoints.py \
  py_packages/validate_agent_files/tests/validate_customizations/test_skill_validation.py \
  py_packages/validate_agent_files/tests/validate_customizations/test_agent_validation.py \
  py_packages/validate_agent_files/tests/validate_customizations/test_prompt_validation.py \
  py_packages/validate_agent_files/tests/validate_skills/test_formatters.py
```

Result: `34 passed`

### Repository Validation

Ran:

```bash
validate_agent_files --recommend .github
```

Result: `37/37 skills valid`

### Not Run

- Full ROS workspace build/test cycle was not rerun for this PR

## Breaking Changes

Yes.

- The validator Python package path changed from `validate_agents` to
`validate_agent_files`
- Legacy internal validator modules were removed

fix: adaptive status polling — 3s when ingesting, 30s when idle

Commit 978d7eb merged status poll (was 2s) + stats poll (was 10s) into
a single 30s interval to reduce overhead. This caused the ingestion
progress badge in the header to not appear for up to 30 seconds after
ingestion started — essentially invisible.

Fix: adaptive interval. Initial fetch is immediate. When isBusy changes
false→true, reschedule at 3s. When isBusy changes true→false, reschedule
at 30s. This keeps the idle overhead low while restoring real-time
ingestion feedback.

fix: illuminate uses compound_id not tags table for content pull

Root cause: tags table only contains tag stub rows (atom_id = tag_atom_id).
Content atom tags live in atoms.tags (JSON column), not the tags table.
Querying tags WHERE tag IN (...) JOIN atoms WHERE source != atom_source
returned 0 rows because every matching row IS a tag atom.

New approach: content atoms have compound_id pointing to their parent
mem_ compound. Query atoms WHERE compound_id IN (<top_hubs>) directly.

Changes:
- Replace fetchContentAtomsByTopTags with fetchContentAtomsByHubs(hubIds)
- Illuminate path skips edge-BFS entirely — goes straight hub → content
- Hub count = seed_count × 20 (default 200 hubs) capped at 500
- Atoms returned in hub-rank order then start_byte (document order)
- Budget trim still applies to limit final output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
